title: 'The Prince'
narrative_elements: performer mistakes crowd applause for personal praise
audience mocks the vain flute player
performer prostrates himself on stage
crowd ejects him for appropriating royal honors

title: Opportunity
narrative_elements: opportunity described as swift and bald
cannot be grasped once passed
even jupiter cannot retrieve lost opportunity

title: The Wolf And The Goat On The Cliff
narrative_elements: wolf pretends concern for goat's safety on cliff
wolf invites goat to come down to meadows
goat recognizes wolf's true motive
goat refuses and stays safe

title: The Man And The Golden Eggs
narrative_elements: goose lays one golden egg per day
man grows wealthy but impatient
man kills goose to get all gold at once
finds nothing inside and loses everything

title: The Wolf And The Crane
narrative_elements: wolf gets bone stuck in throat
wolf promises reward to crane for help
crane removes bone from wolf's throat
wolf refuses payment and claims not killing crane is reward enough

title: The Frog And The Ox
narrative_elements: frog envies ox's size
frog puffs herself up to rival the ox
children report she is still smaller
frog overinflates and bursts dead

title: Zeus And The Good Things
narrative_elements: bad things drive good things to heaven
good things ask zeus how to reach mankind
zeus orders good things to come one at a time
explains why misfortune is common and good fortune rare

title: The Bald Men And The Comb
narrative_elements: bald man finds a comb in the street
another bald man demands to share the find
first man shows it is just a comb useless to both
they lament finding only something useless

title: The Bald Man And The Fly
narrative_elements: fly bites bald man on head
man slaps himself trying to kill the fly
fly mocks man for hurting himself
man defends his action as intentional against the fly

title: The Bald Man And His Two Mistresses
narrative_elements: young mistress plucks all gray hairs
older mistress plucks all black hairs
man believes his looks are improving
man goes completely bald from competing attentions

title: The Bat And The Songbird
narrative_elements: songbird sings only at night after being captured
bat asks why she is silent by day
songbird explains she was captured while singing during daylight
bat notes the caution is useless now that she is already caged

title: The Bat And The Weasels
narrative_elements: bat caught by first weasel who hates birds
bat claims to be a mouse and is released
bat caught by second weasel who hates mice
bat claims to be a bat and is released again

title: The War Between The Beasts And The Birds
narrative_elements: bat switches sides based on who is winning
peace is eventually made between birds and beasts
both sides discover the bat's treachery
bat is condemned to hide in darkness forever

title: The Bat, The Booby And The Bramble Bush
narrative_elements: three form a business partnership and load a ship
ship is wrecked in a storm and cargo lost
bat hides at night to avoid creditors
booby searches sea for copper and bramble grabs cloaks

title: The Bear And The Crabs
narrative_elements: bear cannot find food in woods
bear lowers legs into water near shore
crabs latch onto the bear's fur
bear leaps out and feasts on the crabs

title: The Lion And The Bear
narrative_elements: lion and bear fight over a fawn
both become exhausted from fighting
fox watches them from the side
fox steals the fawn while they rest

title: The Bees And The Beekeeper
narrative_elements: thief steals honeycombs while beekeeper is away
beekeeper discovers empty hives
bees return and sting the innocent beekeeper
beekeeper rebukes bees for misplaced anger

title: The Bees And The Beetles
narrative_elements: bees invite beetles to dinner and offer honey
beetles barely eat anything and leave
beetles invite bees and offer dung
bees refuse to eat and fly home

title: The Bees, The Drones And The Wasps
narrative_elements: drones claim ownership of honeycomb built by bees
wasp judge proposes both parties fill new hives to prove skill
bees agree but drones refuse
judge awards honeycomb to the bees

title: Zeus And The Bee
narrative_elements: bee brings honey to zeus as an offering
bee asks zeus for a sting to defend her honey
zeus grants the sting but warns it will kill the bee
bee accepts the fatal consequence as the price of defense

title: Jupiter And The Fox
narrative_elements: jupiter transforms fox into human queen
fox spots a beetle and leaps to chase it
gods laugh at the fox's instinctive behavior
jupiter expels fox for failing to transcend her nature

title: The Two Dung Beetles
narrative_elements: one beetle leaves for mainland promising to share food
finds plentiful manure on the mainland
returns fat but unable to bring food back
claims the nature of the place prevented him from keeping his promise

title: The Beetle In The Air
narrative_elements: beetle envies the eagle's swift flight
beetle boasts he will fly with the birds
beetle rises into sky but cannot endure the wind
falls far from home and starves

title: Simonides And The Shipwreck
narrative_elements: ship sinks in storm and passengers cling to possessions
simonides refuses to grab belongings saying all he has is with him
passengers lose everything while simonides is recognized and hosted
simonides tells survivors his wisdom was his true wealth

title: The Dogs And The Lion Skin
narrative_elements: dogs find dead lion's skin and tear at it boldly
fox observes their bravado
fox reminds them they would never dare this if lion were alive
comments on hollow courage against the defenseless

title: The Decoys And The Doves
narrative_elements: bird catcher uses tame doves as decoys to trap wild ones
wild doves are caught and angry at tame doves for not warning them
tame doves say loyalty to masters takes precedence over kinship

title: The Bird Catcher And The Partridge
narrative_elements: bird catcher captures a partridge
partridge offers to lure other partridges to save herself
bird catcher is angered by the treacherous offer
bird catcher kills the partridge immediately

title: The Nightingale, The Hawk And The Bird Catcher
narrative_elements: hawk seizes nightingale chicks and demands a song
nightingale sings trembling with fear
hawk eats a chick anyway dismissing the song
bird catcher catches the hawk in a snare

title: The Sparrow And The Myrtle Berries
narrative_elements: sparrow finds sweet myrtle berries and refuses to leave
bird catcher watching captures and kills the sparrow
sparrow laments dying for the sake of momentary pleasure

title: The Bird-Catcher And The Lark
narrative_elements: bird catcher sets a snare and claims to be founding a city
lark is deceived and approaches the bait
lark is trapped in the snare
lark remarks the city will have few inhabitants

title: The Cat And The Birds
narrative_elements: cat invites birds to a birthday party
birds enter cat's house
cat shuts the doors trapping the birds
cat devours birds one after another

title: The Dog And The Blacksmiths
narrative_elements: dog sleeps through loud hammering at the forge
dog wakes immediately at sound of teeth chewing food
blacksmiths confront dog about selective attentiveness

title: The Blacksmiths And The Mouse
narrative_elements: mouse carries corpse of another mouse who starved
blacksmiths laugh at the sight
mouse shames the blacksmiths for not being able to feed even one mouse

title: The Blind Man And The Cub
narrative_elements: blind man identifies animals by touch
given a wolf cub to identify
cannot name it precisely but senses its nature
warns that such a creature should not be near a flock

title: The Old Dog And His Master
narrative_elements: old dog once swift and strong grows feeble with age
dog loses grip on a boar during hunt
hunter angrily scolds the dog
dog asks to be praised for what he once was

title: The Old Lion And The Donkey
narrative_elements: aging lion lies dying and helpless
boar and bull attack to avenge old wounds
donkey kicks lion in the head
lion laments being attacked by the cowardly donkey worst of all

title: The Fox, The Boar And His Tusks
narrative_elements: boar sharpens tusks against a tree when no danger is near
fox asks why he sharpens when there is no immediate need
boar explains he prepares in advance so he is ready when danger comes

title: The Lion And The Boar At The Spring
narrative_elements: lion and boar argue over who drinks first from a spring
fight escalates to a duel to the death
they see vultures waiting to devour the loser
they make peace rather than benefit their common enemies

title: The Lioness And The Wild Boar
narrative_elements: bull kills a sleeping lion
lioness weeps over her dead son
boar stands at safe distance observing
boar reminds lioness how many others weep for sons killed by lions

title: The Drowning Boy
narrative_elements: boy tries to swim in swollen river but is drowning
man stops to lecture the boy for his recklessness
boy pleads for rescue first and lectures later

title: The Farmer'S Boy And The Snails
narrative_elements: boy roasts snails over fire
snails hiss and sizzle in the fire
boy mocks snails for singing while their homes burn

title: The Boys And The Butcher
narrative_elements: one boy steals meat and hides it on the other
both swear innocence when questioned
butcher concludes both boys are guilty even without proof

title: The Fir Tree And The Bramble Bush
narrative_elements: fir tree boasts of height, beauty, and usefulness
bramble bush listens to the boasting
bramble reminds fir that axes constantly chop it down
concludes it is better to be a bramble than a grand but vulnerable tree

title: The Children And The Mirror
narrative_elements: son and daughter discover mirror and son boasts of looks
daughter grows angry at the comparison
she accuses brother of touching women's things
father wisely advises each child to use mirror for self-improvement

title: The Gnat And The Camel
narrative_elements: gnat lands on camel's back and lingers
gnat announces its departure so as not to burden the camel
camel says it did not even notice the gnat's presence

title: The Sick Man And The Gods
narrative_elements: man vows to sacrifice a hundred oxen if healed
gods heal him but he makes fake dough oxen instead
gods promise gold in a dream as retribution
man goes searching and is captured by pirates and enslaved

title: The Bull And The Calf
narrative_elements: bull struggles to fit through narrow entryway with his horns
young calf tries to tell bull how to turn his head
bull tells calf to be quiet as he has done this since before the calf was born

title: The Gnat And The Bull
narrative_elements: gnat challenges bull to test who is stronger
crowd gathers to watch
gnat claims the challenge itself makes them equals
bull should have ignored the gnat rather than dignify it

title: The Bull And The Goats
narrative_elements: bull flees from lion and hides in a cave
wild goats are already inside and start butting the bull
bull tells goats he is not afraid of them
he fears the lion waiting outside not the goats inside

title: The Dolphin And The Lion
narrative_elements: lion proposes alliance with dolphin as king of sea
dolphin agrees to the alliance
lion asks dolphin for help fighting a wild bull
dolphin cannot leave water and explains its natural limits

title: The Two Bulls And The Lion
narrative_elements: lion cannot defeat two united bulls
lion flatters one bull and offers to keep him safe if he betrays the other
one bull turns against his partner
lion defeats both bulls separately

title: The Lion, The Bull And His Horns
narrative_elements: lion fears bull's horns and cannot attack directly
lion flatters bull and suggests horns are a burden
bull is convinced and removes his horns
lion kills the now defenseless bull

title: The Lion And The Two Men
narrative_elements: lion kills a bull and robber demands a share
lion refuses the robber for his greedy habits
innocent traveler backs away in fear
lion invites the modest man to take a portion freely

title: The Dog And The Reeds
narrative_elements: dog tries to relieve himself on the reeds
reed pokes the dog's behind
dog backs off and barks at the reeds
reed tells dog it prefers distant barking to being dirtied

title: The Butcher And The Dog
narrative_elements: dog steals heart from butcher's shop
butcher turns around and discovers the theft
butcher warns dog that it has taught him a lesson
butcher promises to punish the dog if it returns

title: The Monkey And The Camel
narrative_elements: monkey dances and wins approval at animal convention
camel is envious and tries to dance too
camel makes a fool of herself dancing
animals beat the camel and drive her away

title: The Camel And The People
narrative_elements: people first flee in awe of the camel's size
over time they discover the camel is gentle
familiarity breeds contempt
people eventually bridle the camel and let children ride it

title: Zeus And The Camel
narrative_elements: camel sees another animal's horns and begs zeus for horns too
zeus is angered by the camel's greediness
zeus crops the camel's ears as punishment for envy

title: The Night-Owl, The Cat And The Mouse
narrative_elements: owl asks cat to carry him to find someone to talk to
cat brings owl to the mouse's home
mouse comes to the door and recognizes the threat
mouse curses both visitors and refuses to engage

title: The Cat, The Mouse And The Oath
narrative_elements: mouse is trapped in fermented drink and cannot escape
cat rescues mouse in exchange for a promise to come when called
mouse swears an oath
when called later mouse claims he was drunk when he swore

title: The Cat And The Rooster
narrative_elements: cat accuses rooster of waking people at night
rooster defends this as a service
cat accuses rooster of incest
rooster defends this as beneficial for egg production and cat eats him anyway

title: The Cat And The Stork
narrative_elements: cat spots stork carrying an eel and wants it
cat flatters stork about her beautiful red beak
stork refuses to answer keeping her mouth shut
cat insults stork but stork walks on carrying her eel

title: The Eagle, The Sow And The Cat
narrative_elements: cat lives in hollow between eagle's nest and sow's den
cat tells eagle the sow is digging to uproot the tree
cat tells sow the eagle will steal her piglets
both stay put from fear and die of hunger feeding the cat

title: The Man And The Cat
narrative_elements: mouse gnaws at cheese in pantry
man puts cat in pantry to solve mouse problem
cat eats both the mouse and the cheese

title: The Fox And The Goat In The Well
narrative_elements: fox falls into a well and is trapped
thirsty goat arrives and asks if water is good
fox praises the water and invites goat down
fox leaps out on the goat's horns leaving goat behind

title: The Fox And The Stork
narrative_elements: fox invites stork to dinner and serves broth on flat plate
stork cannot eat from the plate and goes hungry
stork invites fox and serves food in a narrow jug
fox cannot reach the food while stork eats freely

title: The Lion And The Man Disputing
narrative_elements: man and lion argue over who is superior
man points to statue of man strangling lion as evidence
lion notes the statue was made by a man
lion shows man at amphitheater that lions actually strangle men

title: The Viper And The File
narrative_elements: viper enters blacksmith's workshop
viper bites the metal file testing if it is edible
file resists and reproaches viper for attacking something that gnaws through iron

title: The Charcoal Burner And The Fuller
narrative_elements: charcoal burner suggests sharing a house with the fuller
fuller refuses as the soot from charcoal would blacken his washed whites
incompatibility of their trades makes cohabitation impossible

title: The Two Men, The Eagle And The Fox
narrative_elements: first man clips eagle's wings and keeps him with chickens
second man buys eagle and restores his feathers
eagle brings hare as gift for second man
fox advises eagle to also appease first man who may hurt him again

title: The Cicada And The Fox
narrative_elements: fox wants to eat cicada and flatters its song
fox invites cicada to come down to be seen
cicada drops a leaf to test the fox
fox pounces on leaf and cicada stays safely in tree

title: The Man And The Insect
narrative_elements: man tries to capture cicada
cicada tells him to hunt birds instead
cicada argues it has no practical value to the man

title: The Eagle And The Farmer
narrative_elements: farmer releases captured eagle out of kindness
eagle sees farmer sitting under collapsing wall
eagle snatches farmer's bandana to lure him away from danger
farmer returns to find wall has collapsed where he sat

title: The Monkey And The Lion's Breath
narrative_elements: lion asks animals if his breath smells bad and kills them regardless of answer
monkey flatters lion saying breath smells of cinnamon
lion spares monkey initially but then plots differently
lion pretends illness and has monkey killed to eat

title: The City Mouse And The Country Mouse
narrative_elements: city mouse visits country and is served humble acorns
city mouse persuades country mouse to visit the city
they feast on delicacies but are interrupted by a butler
country mouse prefers safe acorns to dangerous luxury

title: The Clown, The Farmer And The Pig
narrative_elements: clown perfectly imitates a pig's squeal with no pig hidden
bumpkin challenges clown by hiding a real pig
audience still prefers the clown's performance
bumpkin produces real pig as evidence of crowd's poor judgment

title: Hermes And The Cobblers
narrative_elements: zeus orders hermes to instill deceit in every craftsman
hermes grinds deceit into powder and distributes equal portions
cobbler is last and receives entire leftover dose
result is all craftsmen lie but cobblers are worst

title: The Cobbler And The King
narrative_elements: broke cobbler poses as doctor and gains reputation
king tests him by offering drink claiming it contains poison
cobbler confesses ignorance of medicine to avoid dying
king publicly shames the people for trusting a fraud with their lives

title: The Farmer And The Pig
narrative_elements: pig repeatedly destroys farmer's crops despite punishments
farmer cuts off pig's ears one at a time as warnings
pig continues its destruction and is finally slaughtered
farmer says the pig lacked brains for repeatedly risking its life

title: The Lion, The Cow, The She-Goat And The Sheep
narrative_elements: four animals jointly hunt and kill a large stag
lion claims all four portions through various pretexts
lion uses rank, partnership, and threat to take everything
weaker animals are left with nothing

title: The Coward And The Lion Of Gold
narrative_elements: coward finds a golden lion and is paralyzed by conflicting desires
love of gold conflicts with fear of the lion shape
coward plans to send servants to grab it while watching from distance

title: The Coward And The Ravens
narrative_elements: coward sets out to war
ravens caw and he stops repeatedly
he finally tells ravens to caw all they want
he refuses to let them have his flesh

title: The Crab On Dry Land
narrative_elements: crab leaves sea and comes onto land looking for food
fox sees crab and grabs him
crab laments that it deserves its fate
admits it tried to live on dry land though it is a sea creature

title: The Crane And The Crow
narrative_elements: crane and crow make mutual aid pact to eat farmer's crops
crow predicts farmer's actions based on signals
farmer uses code word to deceive crow's prophecy
farmer stones crane who blames crow for failing him

title: The Crane And The Peacock
narrative_elements: peacock waves golden feathers and boasts of beauty
peacock argues with grey-winged crane
crane points out it can fly to the stars while peacock stays on ground
crane argues freedom of flight surpasses ornamental beauty

title: The Boy And The Scorpion
narrative_elements: boy catches crickets in the desert
boy mistakes scorpion for cricket and reaches for it
scorpion raises sting ready to strike
scorpion warns that touching it would have freed all his captives

title: The Donkey And The Cricket
narrative_elements: donkey hears cricket chirping and loves the sound
donkey asks what food gives cricket its sweet voice
cricket says it lives on air and dew
donkey adopts same diet and dies of hunger

title: The Dogs And The Crocodiles
narrative_elements: dogs drink from the Nile on the run to avoid crocodiles
crocodile tells dog to take his time and not be afraid
dog refuses saying he knows the crocodile craves his flesh

title: The Murderer And The Nile
narrative_elements: murderer flees relatives of victim to the Nile
climbs tree to escape lion but finds snake in tree
throws himself into river to escape snake
crocodile devours him in the river
